scene_summary: A nocturnal observatory reduced to a charcoal field: one dominant matte-black celestial oculus floats high in the frame, leaving broad empty darkness around it. Below center, a warm vellum square translates the Yokota cover’s centered pale panel, heavy black surrounding typography, soft grain, and delicate curling violet-brown line into a quiet star-map fragment. A single oxidized copper spiral arc becomes the second secondary form, crossing the vellum like a handmade orbit. Keep the palette to charcoal pigment, warm vellum, and dark copper, with smoked-glass glints. Visible but small: IMAGE 1983-1998 on the vellum edge, Galaxy DNA etched into the copper curl, Under a Glass Bell as a tiny clear aperture, Taaritt as a sand notch, Fabric as a dim acid-green dial, Circuits as a reed scratch, cam.eee as a profile moon, open world as a low horizon chip, and 13-26 as a date fleck. Silent, spacious, optical.
